latest.title: Monkeys wash food when grit gets annoying
latest.script_key: monkeys can change habits to solve small problems watch the hands near the food because some monkeys learn useful routines from their group washing or handling food differently can remove grit and make eating easier the habit spreads because it works which primate trick should come next

latest.title: Magnets make invisible fields visible
latest.script_key: magnets can show a hidden force map watch the filings line up because each tiny piece becomes a small magnet in the field together they trace the direction of the force around the magnet the pattern is physics drawing itself which force next

latest.title: Orangutans use leaves like rain umbrellas
latest.script_key: orangutans turn forest leaves into tools watch the hands choose the leaf because orangutans are skilled problem solvers a leaf can become cover a glove or a sound tool depending on the moment the forest is full of objects they can repurpose which ape tool next

latest.title: Medieval rider on a tree that's older than knights
latest.script_key: rider fits this tree that s because this oak s rings show it was already 300 years old when the knight rode by notice the deep grooves in the bark those are the tree s fingerprints counting every year would you guess this trunk was already ancient when that armor was shiny

latest.title: Butterflies drink with their feet on flowers
latest.script_key: they taste flowers with their feet watch those feet land then uncurl that s where their taste buds live not their mouth because flowers give off tiny chemical signals that s why butterflies know which blooms to land on next time you see one on a flower look close its feet are doing the tasting

latest.title: Bees don't just pollinate they also 'hear' flowers
latest.script_key: flowers hum to bees see those tiny hairs on a bee s legs those aren t for pollen they re for listening the buzz isn t just noise it s the flower s secret tune that s why bees hit the right bloom every time did you learn this wrong in school too

latest.title: What a dog's tail says before the bark that most people miss
latest.script_key: a dog s tail is a signal not a simple happiness meter watch the first second the body pauses then the tail gives the clue in this clip the recovery angle is happy dog wagging tail most viewers watch the face but the useful signal is smaller that visible cue is the whole story so the short opens on the dog instead of stretching the setup that small detail gives the video a reason to exist

latest.title: Snakes can sense heat when light disappears
latest.script_key: snakes can sense heat when light disappears watch the head before the strike because some snakes have heat sensitive pits that detect warm bodies that gives them a second kind of vision at night the target glows to them without glowing to us which reptile sense next

latest.title: Rock layers store ancient environments in stripes
latest.script_key: rock layers are time stamps made of stone watch the stripe pattern because each layer can mark a different setting river mud ocean floor windblown sand or volcanic ash stack enough layers and the cliff becomes a timeline which rock clue should we read next

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Bull sharks do something you never expected
latest.script_key: here s the part nobody tells you bull sharks in fiji don t hunt alone watch this one twice see the same two sharks cruising the shipwreck again and again they re not just swimming they re keeping the same schedules the same partners for years that s why researchers think they re making friends

latest.title: Bats play the best cricket match you can't see
latest.script_key: watch this bat score its mouth stays closed ears twitch like radar dishes that s because it s not hearing the cricket it s screaming at 100 decibels and listening to the echo the sound bounces off the cricket s wings mid flight so it knows exactly where to swoop that s why the catch looks impossible

latest.title: Trees don’t just drop seeds they shoot them
latest.script_key: watch this one twice see those little pods flipping in the breeze that s not wind that s the tree itself hurling seeds the pods dry out and snap like a tiny catapult because the canopy s crowded they shoot sideways to land in gaps where sunlight hits the ground

latest.title: Coral reefs are cities built by tiny animals
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: Halloween bats hate the dark myth
latest.script_key: bats don t hate the dark it s the echoes they hate that s why they only fly out when it s properly lit so they can hear their own squeaks bounce back watch this one twice their ears twitch to the millisecond now tell me your favorite halloween costume fail

latest.title: Mountains aren’t forever snowcapped but always changing
latest.script_key: even the tallest peaks lie those snowcaps aren t just decoration they re a time bomb glaciers chew rock away faster than the mountain grows i love this one watch how the ice grinds the ridge down like a cheese grater that s why peaks we think are ancient are really getting shorter every year
